name: beehiiv-get-subscriber
description: Look up a Beehiiv subscriber by email address. Returns subscription status, tier, custom fields, tags, and engagement stats. Use to branch on subscriber state before taking further actions.

API_KEY_SECRET.description: Vault path to the Beehiiv API key (e.g. 'beehiiv/api_key').

PUBLICATION_ID.description: Beehiiv publication ID (e.g. 'pub_xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx'). Supports {{key}} template interpolation.

EMAIL.description: Email address to look up. Supports {{key}} template interpolation (e.g. '{{email}}' to pull from upstream node output).

EXPAND.description: Comma-separated list of fields to expand in the response. Options: 'stats' (open_rate, click_through_rate), 'custom_fields', 'tags'. Example: 'stats,custom_fields,tags'. Leave empty for the core subscription object only.

NOT_FOUND_BEHAVIOR.description: 'return_null' (default): returns {found: false} when the subscriber doesn't exist, allowing downstream condition nodes to branch. 'error': fails the node when subscriber is not found.

setup_instructions.title: Create a Beehiiv API key
setup_instructions.detail: Log in to https://app.beehiiv.com, navigate to Settings > Integrations > API, and create a new API key.

setup_instructions.title: Store the API key as a Talos secret
setup_instructions.detail: Use set_secret with key_path='beehiiv/api_key' and value='<your-api-key>'.

setup_instructions.title: Find your Publication ID
setup_instructions.detail: Call GET https://api.beehiiv.com/v2/publications with your API key. Copy the 'id' field (pub_xxx...) for your publication.
